title: vulpia membranacea subvar. occidentalis
intro: Vulpia membranacea subvar. occidentalis, also known as Western Six-angled Puccoon, is a plant species that belongs to the grass family, Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin issues. The health benefits of Western Six-angled Puccoon include its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers, as well as its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce pain and swelling. Western Six-angled Puccoon can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
